notes: Two concurrent kiro-cli chat sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step — a DRIVER GAP record ports first; --trust-all-tools suppresses the per-tool picker). Each kiro process writes its OWN ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl, so the daemon binds each session to the PID owning that cwd via DiscoverPIDByCWD + the PIDManager prefer-unclaimed disambiguator (non-cross-contamination). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready: the process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready before the transcript is discovered and never entering working, so a ready-anchored new_session phase would greedily bind to that placeholder. kiro mints its own session UUID per launch, so the two distinct session_ids are asserted via new_session/same_session_as relations, not literal ids. The .jsonl carries no model/token/cost, so observations assert model unknown, cost zero, tokens zero. Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working->ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session (s2_turn1_start carries new_session:true) does its own working->ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving its PID-to-session binding stayed stable and never flapped to session 2. Phase order follows the recipe: s1 turn -> start_session -> s2 turn -> s1 turn again.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready -> working (anchors session 1's kiro UUID; the pre-session proc-<PID> row never enters working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working -> ready

Session 2 appears in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT kiro UUID and drives its first turn ready -> working — independent of session 1 (new_session proves it is not session 1; anchoring on working skips the born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder)
Session 2 first turn completes: working -> ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original UUID proves the PID-to-session binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working -> ready
